profile_basis: After closing duplicate leading-axis reduction bead frankenjax-mcqr.30.36, br ready had no unclaimed perf bead and active fj-lax/linalg ownership was held by another agent. Read-only recon identified the disjoint fj-dispatch/fj-interpreters dispatch/eval lane. Fresh clean baseline on ts2 at 7ff581c3 showed vmap_scan rows at 51.7-57.0us and dispatch rows at 4.1-40.4us. A broad direct CPU backend resolver was rejected because it regressed vmap dispatch rows despite improving scan rows; this kept lever is scan-local.
lever: For literal-backed and dense-backed scan tensors, choose the storage access path once outside hot scan loops, reuse the borrowed literal or dense i64 slice, and construct accepted i64 scan outputs with TensorValue::new_i64_values. The f32 shared-init scan keeps its literal output representation but hoists the literal slice before the loop. Functional add-emit i64 scan keeps dense outputs and hoists the literal fallback slice outside the batch loop.
baseline_command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/.scratch/targets/frankenjax-dispatch-registry-baseline-7ff cargo bench -p fj-dispatch --bench dispatch_baseline -- 'dispatch_latency|vmap_scan' --sample-size 50 --measurement-time 3 --warm-up-time 1
rebench_command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/.scratch/targets/frankenjax-dispatch-scan-dense-candidate-bench cargo bench -p fj-dispatch --bench dispatch_baseline -- 'vmap_scan' --sample-size 50 --measurement-time 3 --warm-up-time 1
isomorphism_proof: Accepted guards are unchanged: same input arity, shared-init batch_dim constraints, body_op matching, dtype checks, rank checks, expected element counts, and functional add-emit body recognition.

For i64 shared-init add/max, dense and literal inputs use the same row_offset + scan_idx indexing as before. Reverse mode still iterates scan indices in decreasing order. Add remains wrapping_add and max remains signed i64::max.

For f32 shared-init add, the patch only hoists xs.elements.as_slice() outside the loop. Accumulation order, f32 width, NaN behavior, signed zero behavior, and output Literal::F32Bits representation are unchanged.

For functional add-emit i64 scan, dense and literal fallback branches fill final_carry and ys in the same positions as before. Reverse scan still writes each accumulated y at the original row-major scan index.
No RNG, effect ordering, transform ordering, cache-key input, backend selection, dispatch response construction, or error class is changed.

The only i64 representation change is accepted scan outputs moving from Vec<Literal> construction to dense I64 buffers; dtype, shape, batch_dim, extracted values, and observable output ordering are unchanged.
validation: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/.scratch/targets/frankenjax-dispatch-scan-dense-test cargo test -p fj-dispatch --lib batch_trace_scan

RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/.scratch/targets/frankenjax-dispatch-scan-dense-func-test cargo test -p fj-dispatch --lib batch_eval_jaxpr_scan_sub_jaxprs
RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/.scratch/targets/frankenjax-dispatch-scan-dense-check cargo check -p fj-dispatch --all-targets

RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/.scratch/targets/frankenjax-dispatch-scan-dense-clippy-nodeps cargo clippy -p fj-dispatch --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
validation_notes: A stricter dependency-linting run without --no-deps failed in existing fj-core code at crates/fj-core/src/lib.rs:678 with clippy::nonminimal_bool. The touched fj-dispatch targets pass clippy with --no-deps, and cargo check -p fj-dispatch --all-targets passes.
All performance baselines and rebenchmarks listed above ran on worker ts2.
